rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement are coherent and well posed.
rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section requires checking whether the motivation, method description, evaluation, and conclusion form a consistent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and cost claims, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims align with the reported benchmarks and numbers.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains a strong first-work contribution claim, making novelty assessment a central responsibility of a substantive review.
rationale.R1.6: A request to review a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The review must disclose uncertainty where the artifact does not provide enough detail to verify claims fully.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against overstated claims such as heavy-detector quality, negligible accuracy loss, and firstness.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or expanding the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid enabling misleading scientific communication or unsupported deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: Any observations should be traceable to specific wording, claims, or reported results in the section.
